children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: The Secretary shall administer the Wilderness Area in accordance with this part and the Wilderness Act ( et seq.). Any reference in the Wilderness Act to the effective date of that Act (or any similar reference) shall be deemed to be a reference to .16 U.S.C. 1131October 30, 2000

children.heading: Wilderness boundaries along roads
children.label: b Wilderness boundaries along roads
children.text: Where a wilderness boundary exists along a road, the wilderness boundary shall be set back from the centerline of the road, consistent with the Bureau of Land Management’s guidelines as established in its Wilderness Management Policy.

children.heading: Access to non-Federal lands
children.label: c Access to non-Federal lands
children.text: The Secretary shall provide reasonable access to private lands within the boundaries of the Wilderness Area, as provided in section 460nnn–22(d) of this title.1So in original. Probably should be section “460nnn–22(e)”.1

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 1 Administration
children.text: Except as provided in , grazing of livestock shall be administered in accordance with the provision of section 4(d)(4) of the Wilderness Act (), in accordance with the provisions of this subchapter, and in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Appendices A and B of House Report 101–405 of the 101st Congress.section 460nnn–23(e)(2) of this title16 U.S.C. 1133(d)(4)

children.heading: Retirement of certain permits
children.label: 2 Retirement of certain permits
children.text: The Secretary shall permanently retire all grazing permits applicable to certain lands in the Wilderness Area, as depicted on the map referred to in , and livestock shall be excluded from these lands.section 460nnn–11(a) of this title
